name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Access Key Creation for Another User (AWS)
notes:
This Building Block defines a user creating an access key for another user. In AWS, a user with "iam:CreateAccessKey" permission can create access keys for a user they have access to. An attacker can potentially gain access to an admin user.

Note: AWS access keys are credentials used for requests to AWS CLI or API.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Access Key Creation for Another User (AWS) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
when the event QID is one of the following (88750119) Create Access Key
when the event matches "Username" != "Target Username" AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Access Key Creation for Another User (AWS) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
AND when the event QID is one of the following (88750119) Create Access Key
AND when the event matches "Username" != "Target Username" AQL filter query
